    'subject' => 'System Notification',
    'message' => '<h1>Update!</h1><p>Your content has been published.</p>',
    'msgtype' => 'html'
];

$result = Mail::send($vars);
if ($result !== true) {
    Log::error("Mail failed: " . $result);
}

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuration Schema
docs.text:
The behavior of the Mail engine is governed by the settings defined in Settings > E-Mail.

  • mailtype: Toggle between 0 (Local PHP Mail) and 1 (External SMTP Relay).
  • smtphost: The address of your SMTP provider (e.g., smtp.gmail.com).
  • smtpport: The connection port (usually 465 for SSL or 587 for TLS).
  • smtpuser: Your authentication username/email.
  • smtppass: Your authentication password.

docs.title: 🏗️ Internal Workflow
docs.text:
The Mail class performs several automated steps during transmission: 1. Configuration Fetch: Retrieves all SMTP and Site parameters from the Options table. 2. Engine Boot: Instantiates the PHPMailer object with the appropriate encryption (SSL/TLS). 3. Sender Identification: Automatically uses the site's default name and email as the "From" identity. 4. Error Handling: Wraps the transmission in an exception block, returning the raw error if the server rejects the request.

[!IMPORTANT] SMTP Delivery: For high-volume or critical transactional emails (like password resets), it is strongly recommended to use a dedicated SMTP service (SendGrid, Mailgun, or Gmail) rather than the local PHP mail() function to avoid being flagged as spam.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• E-Mail User Guide — Configuring mail delivery in the dashboard.
  • Vendor Class — How PHPMailer is integrated into the system.

docs.title: Navigation Menu Class
docs.text: The Menus class is the primary engine for constructing, managing, and rendering site-wide navigation systems in GeniXCMS. It supports multi-level nested hierarchies (up to 4 levels) and features intelligent link resolution for static pages, categories, and module-based URLs.

docs.title: Menus::getMenu(string $menuid, ...)
docs.text:
The primary method for injecting a navigation structure into your theme's templates.

Parameter Type Default Description $menuid string Required The unique identifier matching the database (e.g., mainmenu). $class string '' Custom CSS class appended to the root <ul> element. $bsnav bool false If true, it generates Bootstrap 5 compatible dropdown markup.

Returns: string (HTML fragment).

{* In your Latte theme/template *}
{Menus::getMenu('top-menu', 'navbar-nav ms-auto', true)|noescape}

docs.title: 🏗️ Management & Data Access
docs.text:
The class also provides several utilities for retrieving and manipulating menu items programmatically.

  • Menus::getMenuRaw(string $menuid): Returns an unformatted array of menu objects for the specified ID.
  • Menus::id(int $id): Retrieves a specific menu item object by its primary key.
  • Menus::getMenuAdmin(string $menuid): Generates the interactive, drag-and-drop management interface used in the administration dashboard.

docs.title: 🛠️ CRUD Operations
docs.text:
These methods are typically used within the administrative controllers to handle the navigation lifecycle.

Method Description Menus::insert(array $vars) Dispatches a new menu item to the database. Menus::update(array $vars) Modifies existing item parameters like labels, parents, or URLs. Menus::delete(int $id) Permanently removes an item and its children. Menus::updateMenuOrder(array $vars) Updates the vertical stacking order based on drag-and-drop feedback.

docs.title: 🔗 Intelligent Link Resolution
docs.text:
The Menus class integrates deeply with the Url Class, allowing it to automatically resolve the correct destination based on the item type (e.g., Page, Post, Category, or Mod). This ensures that your navigation remains functional even if you switch between Standard and Smart URL modes.

[!TIP] Nesting Limit: While the current engine is hardcoded to support up to 4 levels of nesting, it is recommended for UX purposes to keep your primary navigations to 2 or 3 levels for maximum mobile responsiveness and accessibility.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Menus User Guide — Visually building navigations in the dashboard.
  • Url Class — How the system generates destination links for menu items.

docs.title: Migration Builder Class
docs.text: The Migration class is the foundation of GeniXCMS's database versioning system. It allows developers to define schema changes (tables, columns, indexes) in PHP code, ensuring consistent database states across all development and production environments.

docs.title: ⚙️ How it Works
docs.text:
Each migration is a standalone PHP file containing a class that implements two critical directions:

  • up(): Defines the changes to be applied (e.g., CREATE TABLE).
  • down(): Defines how to reverse those changes (e.g., DROP TABLE).

docs.title: 🏗️ Creating a Migration
docs.text: Migrations are stored in inc/migrations/ and must follow a strict naming and class structure to be discovered by the engine.

docs.title: 📂 File Naming Convention
docs.text:
The filename must follow the pattern: YYYYMMDD-batchid-Description.php

  • Example: 20240327-01-CreatePermissionsTable.php

docs.title: 📂 Class Naming Convention
docs.text:
The class name must match the filename, replacing dashes with underscores and prefixing with Migration_.

  • Example: class Migration_20240327_01_CreatePermissionsTable extends Migration

docs.title: 🚀 Execution via CLI
docs.text:
The genix console provides built-in commands to manage your migration stack.

Command Description php genix migrate Scans for pending files and executes their up() methods in chronological order. php genix migrate:rollback Identifies the last execution batch and runs their down() methods to undo changes.

docs.title: 📝 Example Implementation
docs.text:
<?php

class Migration_20240327_10_AddProductTable extends Migration
{
    /**
     * Apply schema changes.
     */
    public function up()
    {
        $sql = "CREATE TABLE `products` (
            `id` int(11) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
            `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
            `price` decimal(10,2) NOT NULL
        ) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8;";

        Db::query($sql);
    }

    /**
     * Revert schema changes.
     */
    public function down()
    {
        Db::query("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `products` ");
    }
}

docs.title: ⚡ Method Reference
docs.text:
• Migration::init(): System internal. Ensures the migrations tracking table exists.
  • Migration::run(): Dispatches the migration execution logic.
  • Migration::rollback(): Dispatches the rollback logic.

[!IMPORTANT] Data Safety: Always double-check your down() method. A well-written rollback is just as important as the migration itself to prevent manual database cleanup during failed deployments.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• CLI Console — Full list of genix commands.
  • Db Class — Understanding how to execute raw SQL within migrations.

docs.title: Module Management Class
docs.text: The Mod class is the primary orchestrator for the GeniXCMS extension ecosystem. It manages the entire lifecycle of modules (plugins), from discovery and metadata extraction to activation, deactivation, and administrative interface integration.

docs.title: Mod::activate(string $module_slug)
docs.text:
Registers a module in the system's active registry. This method is called when an administrator clicks "Install/Activate" in the dashboard.

  • Process: Triggers optional database migrations and attaches core hooks.
  • Return: bool (Success of activation).

docs.title: Mod::deactivate(string $module_slug)
docs.text: Unregisters the module from the active list. The module files remain on the server, but all hooks and administrative links are suppressed.

docs.title: Mod::isActive(string $module_slug)
docs.text:
A high-frequency utility method used throughout the system to check if a specific feature set is available.

if (Mod::isActive('contact-form')) {
    // Render contact form logic...
}

docs.title: Mod::data(string $module_slug)
docs.text:
Parses the header comment from a module's index.php to extract versioning, author, and description data.

/**
 * Name: My Engine
 * Version: 2.1.0
 * Developer: GeniXCMS Team
 */

$meta = Mod::data('my-engine');
echo $meta['Name'];     // Output: My Engine
echo $meta['Version'];  // Output: 2.1.0

docs.title: 🎨 Administrative Integration
docs.text: The Mod class handles how modules appear and behave within the GeniXCMS dashboard.

docs.title: Mod::addMenuList(array $menus)
docs.text:
Allows modules to register custom administration pages that appear in the Admin > Modules dropdown or sidebar.

// Typically called in the module constructor
$pages = [
    'analytics' => 'Site Traffic Analytics',
    'reports'   => 'Monthly Sales'
];
Mod::addMenuList($pages);

docs.title: Mod::options(string $module_slug)
docs.text: Automatically includes the options.php file for a specific module, allowing for a centralized settings experience without manual include calls.

docs.title: Mod::inc(string $file, ...)
docs.text:
A secure loader for module-specific internal libraries and views.

  • Purpose: Prevents path resolution issues across different server environments.
  • Usage: Mod::inc('MyLibrary.lib', $data, 'inc/').

[!TIP] Developer Quick Start: To start building a module with all required structures already in place, use the GeniX CLI: php genix make:module my-new-feature

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Module User Guide — Managing extensions from the dashboard.
  • How to Create a Module — Technical specifications for developers.
  • Hooks Class — How modules attach to the core system.

docs.title: Model Class (ORM / ActiveRecord)
docs.text: The Model class provides a robust, ActiveRecord-style ORM for interacting with your database. It allows you to treat database rows as native PHP objects, inheriting complex data manipulation capabilities like finding, saving, updating, and deleting with minimal boilerplate code.

docs.title: 🏗️ Creating Your Model
docs.text:
To create a domain entity, simply extend the Model class and define your target table name.

/**
 * Example: Custom Book Model
 */
class Books extends Model 
{
    protected $table = 'books';
    protected $primaryKey = 'id'; // Default is 'id'
}

docs.title: 🔍 Finding & Retrieving Records
docs.text: The Model class features a fluent interface for data retrieval.

docs.title: Model::find(int $id)
docs.text:
Quickly retrieve a single record by its primary key. Returns a model instance or null.

$book = Books::find(12);
if ($book) {
    echo $book->title;
}

docs.title: Model::all()
docs.text: Returns a collection (array of objects) containing all records from the table.

docs.title: 💾 Saving & Updating Data
docs.text: The save() method intelligently determines whether to execute an INSERT or an UPDATE based on the existence of the primary key.

docs.title: Creating New Records
docs.text:
// Instantiate and set properties
$book = new Books();
$book->title = "The Art of CMS";
$book->author = "GeniXCMS Team";
$book->save(); // Performs INSERT

// Or use the static shorthand
Books::create([
    'title' => 'Fast Lane',
    'status' => '1'
]);

docs.title: Updating Existing Records
docs.text:
$book = Books::find(12);
$book->title = "Updated Title";
$book->save(); // Performs UPDATE

docs.title: ⚡ Chainable Query Integration
docs.text:
Models integrate natively with the Query Builder, allowing you to start complex queries directly from your class.

// Find specific records using chaining
$active_books = Books::where('status', '1')
    ->orderBy('date', 'desc')
    ->limit(10)
    ->get();

docs.title: 📝 Technical Method Summary
docs.text: Method Role Return Type all() Retrieve all table records. array<objects> find($id) Look up a single record by its PK. Model \| null where() Initiate a fluent query condition. QueryBuilder create($data) Static shorthand for immediate insertion. bool save() Persistent save (Insert/Update) of the current object. bool delete() Permanent removal of the current object. bool

docs.title: 🔮 Dynamic Attributes
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses PHP Magic Methods (__get and __set) to map database columns to object properties. This ensures your code remains clean and intuitive.

$post = Posts::find(42);
echo $post->content; // Automatically resolves to the 'content' column

[!IMPORTANT] Data Integrity: When using Model::save(), ensure your table's primary key is correctly defined in the $primaryKey property if it deviates from the standard id. Failure to do so may result in duplicate records.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Query Builder — Mastering complex SQL queries through the model layer.
  • Db Class — Direct database interaction for non-model logic.

docs.title: Global Options & Settings Class
docs.text: The Options class is the central repository for site-wide configurations in GeniXCMS. It manages the persistent storage and efficient retrieval of everything from core metadata (Site Name, E-mail) to granular plugin parameters and theme-specific settings.

docs.title: ⚡ Data Retrieval Patterns
docs.text: Fetching configuration data must be efficient to ensure sub-second page loads. GeniXCMS provides two distinct retrieval pathways.

docs.title: Options::v(string $key)
docs.text:
This is the High-Performance method. It retrieves values from the system's pre-loaded data object (internal cache), which is initialized during the core boot phase.

  • Best Use Case: Use this in themes and templates to avoid redundant database queries.
  • Example: <?= Options::v('site_name'); ?>

docs.title: Options::get(string $key, bool $decode = true)
docs.text:
This method performs a Direct Database Query for the specified key.

  • Best Use Case: Use this when you need to ensure you have the most up-to-the-second value, or when fetching large JSON objects from modules.
  • Example: $raw_json = Options::get('my_module_config');

docs.title: 💾 Saving & Modifying Data
docs.text: The Options engine supports both single-key updates and batch processing.

docs.title: Options::update($input, $value = '')
docs.text:
The primary method for editing site configuration.

Scenario Input Type Usage Example Single Key string Options::update('site_email', 'new@site.com'); Batch Update array Options::update(['A' => '1', 'B' => '2']);

docs.title: Options::insert(array $vars)
docs.text: Dispatches multiple new options to the database simultaneously. Typically used during module installation or theme activation.

docs.title: 🔍 Validation & Existence
docs.text:
Before attempting to use or update a setting, you can verify its existence.

  • Options::isExist(string $key): Returns a bool indicating if the key is present in the database.
  • Options::validate(string $key): Checks if the key exists and has a non-empty value.

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Method Summary
docs.text:
Method Role Return Type v($key) Fetch from pre-loaded internal cache. mixed get($key) Direct database retrieval. mixed update() Modify existing option(s). bool insert() Add multiple new options. bool isExist() Check for key presence. bool

[!TIP] Performance Hint: Always prioritize Options::v() within your theme's header.php or footer.php. This method allows GeniXCMS to serve the entire site configuration with zero additional SQL overhead after the initial boot.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• System Configuration Guide — Managing these values via the dashboard.
  • OptionsBuilder Guide — Creating automated settings panels for themes.

docs.title: OptionsBuilder Class (UI Engine)
docs.text: The OptionsBuilder class is a specialized UI utility in GeniXCMS 2.0.0-alpha designed to construct professional, high-performance administration settings panels for themes and modules. It abstracts complex HTML rendering, handles data persistence, and features an integrated Dynamic CSS Engine for automated frontend styling.

docs.title: ✨ Integrated Engine Features
docs.text:
• 📂 Visual Tabbed Interface: Automatically organizes settings into logical tabs and sidebar groups with zero manual HTML.
  • 🎨 Rich Component Library: Native support for color pickers, range sliders, toggles, and complex typography groups.
  • ⚡ Dynamic CSS Generator: Automatically generates <style> blocks and manages Google Fonts injection based on user selections.
  • 📱 Responsive Grid Layout: Built-in support for 2 and 3 column responsive field grids.

docs.title: 🏗️ Implementing an Admin Panel
docs.text:
To use the builder in your theme's options.php or a module's settings interface:

// 1. Prepare your data repository
$opt = json_decode(Options::get('modern_theme_opts'), true) ?? [];

// 2. Define the UI Schema (Tabs -> Cards -> Fields)
$schema = [
    [
        'id'       => 'tab-branding',
        'label'    => 'Branding',
        'icon'     => 'bi bi-palette',
        'group'    => 'Design',
        'title'    => 'Theme Appearance',
        'cards'    => [
            [
                'title'  => 'Color Palette',
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'color', 'name' => 'primary_color', 'label' => 'Accent'],
                    ['type' => 'toggle', 'name' => 'dark_mode', 'label' => 'Enable Dark Mode'],
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

// 3. Instantiate and Render
$builder = new OptionsBuilder($opt, [], [], [
    'brandName' => 'Modern Nexus',
    'saveKey'   => 'save_options_btn'
]);
$builder->render($schema);

docs.title: 📝 Available UI Components
docs.text:
The builder supports a wide range of specialized field types:

Type Visual Representation Best Use Case color Hex + Visual Picker Theme accent and background colors. range Slider with Value Badge Spacing, font sizes, and opacity. toggle iOS-style Switch Feature toggles and dark mode switches. typo_row Composite Typography Comprehensive font family, size, and weight management. select Styled Dropdown Theme layout or font family selection. select_media Integrated Media Picker Selecting logos or background images from the library.

docs.title: 🚀 Dynamic CSS Generation
docs.text:
The builder can automatically generate and minify CSS for your theme's frontend, including intelligent asynchronous loading of Google Fonts.

// In your theme's header.php or NexusTheme::styles()
echo OptionsBuilder::generateFrontendCSS($opt, [
    'themeUrl' => Url::theme(),
    'minify'   => true,
    'id'       => 'theme-custom-styles'
]);

docs.title: ⚡ Class Reference Summary
docs.text:
• __construct(array $opt, ...): Initializes the engine with saved data and configuration parameters.
  • render(array $schema): Computes and outputs the full HTML for the administrative panel.
  • static generateFrontendCSS(array $opt, ...): Static utility to return <link> tags and a <style> block for the frontend.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: When using generateFrontendCSS(), the builder automatically caches the results. This ensures that even complex typography and color mappings do not impact your site's Time-To-First-Byte (TTFB).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Theme Development Guide — How to integrate OptionsBuilder into a custom theme.
  • Options Class — How data is persistently stored in the database.

docs.title: Page Type & Localization Class
docs.text: The Pages class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS designed to identify the current content context within your theme's templates. It provides a semantic set of conditional methods that allow your design to adapt dynamically based on whether a visitor is viewing the homepage, a blog post, a static page, or a custom module.

docs.title: ⚡ Content Context Discovery
docs.text:
The following methods are the primary tool for theme logic, returning a bool based on the active route's detected type.

Method Returns true if current view is... Pages::isHome() The site's primary landing page (Index). Pages::isPost() An individual editorial blog post. Pages::isPage() A foundational static page (e.g., About Us). Pages::isCat() A category-based content archive. Pages::isTag() A tag-based content archive. Pages::isMod() A custom page provided by an active module.

docs.title: 🎨 Implementation Workflow
docs.text:
These context-aware methods are typically used in theme files (like sidebar.php, header.php, or footer.php) to toggle layouts or inject specialized meta-tags and assets.

// Example: Dynamic Sidebar Selection
if (Pages::isPost()) {
    // Inject blog-specific components (Author Bio, Related Posts)
    Theme::inc('sidebars/blog-meta'); 
} elseif (Pages::isPage()) {
    // Inject page-specific components (Table of contents, etc.)
    Theme::inc('sidebars/page-navigation');
}

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Engine
docs.text:
The Pages class functions as a thin wrapper around the global $data array.

  • Initialization: The context is determined by the Control Class immediately after the Router resolves the URI.
  • Population: The framework populates the p_type key, which these methods monitor.

docs.title: 💡 Advanced Layout Switching
docs.text:
Combined with the Theme Class, you can create a completely polymorphic site architecture.

// In your theme's index.php
if (Pages::isHome()) {
    Theme::inc('layouts/home-hero');
} else {
    Theme::inc('layouts/subpage-header');
}

[!TIP] SEO Performance: Use Pages::isHome() in your header to decide whether to wrap your site name in an <h1> (on the home page) or an <a> (on sub-pages) for optimal semantic SEO hierarchy.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Theme Class — Dynamically including design components.
  • Router Class — How the system determines the page type from the URL.
  • User Guide: Pages — Managing static pages in the dashboard.

docs.title: Content Pagination Class
docs.text: The Paging class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS designed to build structural pagination links for both the frontend content archives and the administrative management tables. It handles complex record counting, page calculation, and Bootstrap-compliant HTML generation in a single method call.

docs.title: Paging::create(array $vars, bool $smart_url = false)
docs.text:
Generates a complete, semantic HTML pagination fragment based on the provided configuration.

Parameter Type Default Description $vars array Required Associative configuration for record count and URL patterns. $smart_url bool false If true, it generates SEO-friendly URL segments (e.g., /paging/2/).

Returns: string (HTML fragment).

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuration Schema ($vars)
docs.text: Key Example Description table 'posts' Target database table to calculate total record volume. where 'status=1' Optional SQL WHERE clause for granular filtering. total 100 Optional manual count (overrides automated table query). max 10 Maximum items displayed per individual page. url Site::$url Base URL for the pagination assembly. paging 1 Current active page integer. type 'number' Display style: 'number' (pagination list) or 'pager' (Prev/Next).

docs.title: Professional Numbered List (Bootstrap)
docs.text:
Ideal for standard blog archives and administrative data grids.

$vars = [
    'table' => 'posts',
    'where' => "type='post' AND status='1'",
    'max'   => 12,
    'url'   => 'https://site.com/blog/',
    'paging'=> 1,
    'type'  => 'number'
];

echo Paging::create($vars, true);

docs.title: Simple "Previous/Next" Pager
docs.text:
Ideal for minimalist themes or sequential content flows.

$vars = [
    'table' => 'posts',
    'max'   => 20,
    'url'   => 'index.php?page=archives',
    'paging'=> 1,
    'type'  => 'pager'
];

echo Paging::create($vars);

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Workflow
docs.text:
The Paging class operates through several automated phases:

  1. Metric Calculation: It performs a Db::num_rows() query on the provided table/where condition to determine the data universe size.
  2. Logic Partition: Calculates the total page count by dividing the universe size by the max value.
  3. URL Assembly: Decodes whether to use standard query parameters (?paging=X) or SEO-friendly segments (/paging/X/).
  4. HTML Synthesis: Built-in support for Bootstrap styling, injecting active and disabled CSS classes to individual page nodes.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: Always enable the $smart_url = true parameter for public-facing content lists. This ensures search engine crawlers can easily index your deep content archives through clean, predictable URL paths.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Router Class — How the system handles the /paging/ URL segment.
  • Db Class — How the Paging engine queries the record volume.

docs.title: Post Metadata & Custom Fields Class
docs.text: The Params class is the primary extensibility framework for the GeniXCMS content editor. It allows modules to inject custom input fields into the Post and Page management screens, automatically handling the rendering, retrieval, and mapping of metadata (Custom Fields) to specific content IDs.

docs.title: ⚡ Registering Custom Field Groups
docs.text: Modules can register groups of fields to be displayed in either the Sidebar (compact) or the Bottom (expanded) sections of the editor form.

docs.title: Params::register(array $config)
docs.text:
The core registration method used within a module's constructor or initialization hook.

$custom_meta = [
    'sidebar' => [ // Location: 'sidebar' or 'bottom'
        [
            'groupname'  => 'product_specification',
            'grouptitle' => 'E-Commerce Details',
            'fields'     => [
                [
                    'name'     => 'item_sku', 
                    'title'    => 'SKU Number',
                    'type'     => 'text',
                    'boxclass' => 'col-md-12'
                ],
                [
                    'name'     => 'item_condition',
                    'title'    => 'Condition',
                    'type'     => 'dropdown',
                    'value'    => ['New', 'Refurbished', 'Used'],
                    'boxclass' => 'col-md-12'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

Params::register($custom_meta);

docs.title: 🏗️ Supported Component Types
docs.text:
The Params engine supports a variety of standard UI components for data entry:

Component Type Configuration Best Use Case text Default Identifiers, SKU, prices, or external IDs. textarea Default Short descriptions or custom code snippets. checkbox Default Feature toggles (e.g., "Feature on Home"). dropdown Needs value array Statuses, categories, or predefined colors.

docs.title: 🔄 Technical Data Lifecycle
docs.text:
The Params class operates as an automated bridge between your module and the Posts model.

  1. Rendering: Hooks into post_param_form_sidebar and post_param_form_bottom.
  2. Hydration: Upon editor load, it automatically retrieves existing values using Posts::getParam($name, $post_id).
  3. Persistence: Field names are automatically prefixed. The core Posts::save() routine detects these parameters and commits them to the postparam table during the primary save transaction.

docs.title: 🛠️ Direct Rendering Utilities
docs.text:
While usually automated via hooks, developers can manually trigger metadata form rendering.

  • Params::renderSidebar(): Compiles and outputs all registered sidebar groups.
  • Params::renderBottom(): Compiles and outputs all registered bottom-area groups.

[!TIP] Developer Hint: To retrieve your custom parameters in a theme's single.php, use the Posts class helper: $price = Posts::getParam('product_price', $id);

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — How parameters are persistently stored and retrieved.
  • Module Creation Guide — How to use Params within a custom extension.

docs.title: Search Engine Notification Class (Pinger)
docs.text: The Pinger class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS that automates the process of notifying search engines, aggregators, and directory services whenever new content is published or existing content is updated. It utilizes the Weblog XML-RPC Ping protocol to ensure your site's changes are indexed as rapidly as possible.

docs.title: Pinger::run($services)
docs.text:
Dispatches a notification to one or more directory services.

Parameter Type Required Description $services array\|string Yes A target URL list (array or newline-separated string).

Internal Logic: The method automatically extracts the site name, homepage URL, and RSS feed URL from the system settings to populate the ping payload.

// Dispatch to a specific list of engines
$engines = [
    'rpc.pingomatic.com',
    'blogsearch.google.com/ping/RPC2'
];

Pinger::run($engines);

docs.title: Pinger::isOn()
docs.text:
Verifies if the automated ping systems are enabled in the administrative settings.

  • Returns: bool (True if pinger_enable is set to "Yes").

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The Pinger class leverages the integrated IXR Library (Incubator XML-RPC) for network transport and XML formatting.

  1. Payload Discovery: Gathers sitename, siteurl, and rssurl from global options.
  2. Multicast Handing: If a newline-separated string of services is provided, the class automatically explodes the string into a processable array.
  3. RPC Transmission:
    • weblogUpdates.extendedPing: First, it attempts an extended ping for richer metadata.
    • weblogUpdates.ping: Falls back to a basic ping if the target service does not support extended protocols.

[!IMPORTANT] Network Requirements: For the Pinger to function correctly, your server must have cURL enabled and be able to make outbound requests to external XML-RPC endpoints on the public web.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: In the admin dashboard under Settings > General, ensure you provide a comprehensive list of ping services (like Pingomatic) to maximize your site's organic discovery speed after every new blog post.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Vendor Class — How the IXR Library is integrated into the core.
  • Posts Class — Where the Pinger is typically triggered after a successful save.

docs.title: Core Content (Posts & Pages) Class
docs.text: The Posts class is the primary engine for content management in GeniXCMS. It provides a comprehensive interface for creating, modifying, and retrieving all editorial content types, including blog posts, static pages, and custom module-driven content.

docs.title: Posts::insert(array $vars)
docs.text:
Dispatches a new content record to the database.

  • Automation: It automatically generates a unique, SEO-friendly slug based on the title and handles the creation of a revision history.
  • Payload: Requires keys like title, content, author, type, and status.
$vars = [
    'title'   => 'The Future of GeniXCMS',
    'cat'     => 5,
    'content' => 'Full editorial content here...',
    'author'  => 'admin',
    'type'    => 'post',
    'status'  => '1'
];
Posts::insert($vars);

docs.title: Posts::update(array $vars)
docs.text: Modifies an existing record. The method automatically detects the ID and applies changes to the provided keys only.

docs.title: Posts::recent(array $config)
docs.text:
Retrieves a filtered collection of recent content.

$config = [
    'num'  => 5,      // Number of records
    'type' => 'post', // 'post' or 'page'
    'cat'  => 1       // Filter by category ID
];
$collection = Posts::recent($config);

docs.title: 📝 Format & Rendering Engine
docs.text:
These methods handle the transformation of raw database content into presentation-ready HTML.

Method Role Logic content($raw) Rendering Block Cleans content, processes Shortcodes, and handles the [[--readmore--]] tag. format($raw, $id) Archive Snippet Truncates content at the "Read More" tag and appends a permalinked "Read More" button. title($id) Identity Resolves the plain-text title for a specific record.

docs.title: Posts::getPostImage(int $id)
docs.text: Retrieves the featured image path associated with the record through its metadata.

docs.title: Posts::getImage(string $content, int $n = 1)
docs.text: An intelligent parser that scans the content body and extracts the URL of the Nth image found. This is frequently used for automated thumbnail generation from content.

docs.title: 🏷️ Metadata Management (Post Parameters)
docs.text:
The Posts class provides a robust PostParam system for handling custom fields and metadata.

  • addParam($key, $val, $id): Commits a new metadata key-value pair.
  • editParam($key, $val, $id): Updates existing metadata.
  • getParam($key, $id): Retrieves a specific metadata value.
  • delParam($key, $id): Permanent removal of a specific key.
// Example: Getting a custom SEO title from metadata
$seo_title = Posts::getParam('seo_title', $post_id);

[!TIP] Read More Support: To create an "Excerpt" in your content manager, simply insert [[--readmore--]] into your HTML. The Posts::format() method will then ensure your archive pages display a clean summary instead of the full article.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Pages Class — Helper methods to detect current content context.
  • Params Class — Registering custom fields that map to the PostParam system.
  • User Guide: Posts — Managing editorial content from the dashboard.

docs.title: Query Builder (Fluent API)
docs.text: The Query class provides a fluent, chainable interface for building and executing database queries in GeniXCMS. It improves code readability while automatically handling security via PDO prepared statements for all bound parameters.

docs.title: 🚀 Basic Initialization
docs.text:
The entry point for any operation is the table() static method.

// Targets the 'posts' table
$query = Query::table('posts');

docs.title: select($columns)
docs.text:
Define the dataset you wish to retrieve. Accepts a string of coma-separated columns or an array.

Type Example All Columns Query::table('posts')->get(); (Default) Specific Column Query::table('posts')->select('title')->get(); Array Selection Query::table('posts')->select(['id', 'title'])->get();

docs.title: where($column, $operator, $value)
docs.text:
Adds a standard WHERE constraint. If the operator is omitted, = is used by default.

// Equality check
$active = Query::table('posts')->where('status', '1')->get();

// Relational operators
$recent = Query::table('posts')->where('date', '>', '2024-01-01')->get();

docs.title: orWhere($column, $operator, $value)
docs.text:
Chains an additional constraint using the OR logic.

$posts = Query::table('posts')
    ->where('type', 'post')
    ->orWhere('type', 'page')
    ->get();

docs.title: join($table, $first, $operator, $second, $type = 'INNER')
docs.text:
Fluent interface for combining related tables.

$results = Query::table('posts')
    ->select('posts.*, user.username')
    ->join('user', 'posts.author', '=', 'user.userid')
    ->get();

docs.title: 🔢 Ordering and Pagination
docs.text:
Method Parameters Description orderBy $column, $direction Sort results (ASC/DESC). limit $count, $offset Restrict number of rows and set starting point. 
// Example: Get 10 latest posts, skipping the first 5
$paged = Query::table('posts')
    ->orderBy('date', 'DESC')
    ->limit(10, 5)
    ->get();

docs.title: insert(array $data)
docs.text:
Creates a new record. 

Query::table('posts')->insert([
    'title'  => 'New Entry',
    'status' => '1'
]);

docs.title: update(array $data)
docs.text:
Modifies existing records matching the criteria. 

Query::table('posts')
    ->where('id', 5)
    ->update(['status' => '0']);

docs.title: ⚡ Execution Methods
docs.text:
• get(): Returns an array of objects containing all matched records.
  • first(): Returns the first object found, or null if empty.
  • count(): Returns the integer count of records matching the query.

[!TIP] Performance: For simple, single-row operations where you need object mapping, consider using the Model Layer. Use Query for complex JOINs and aggregations.

docs.title: Headless CMS & RESTful API Engine
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0-alpha features a powerful, built-in RESTful API Engine that allows you to use the platform as a Headless CMS. This architecture enables you to decouple your content management from your frontend delivery, serving your data to mobile apps, static site generators (like Hugo or Gatsby), or custom JavaScript frameworks.

docs.title: 🔐 Administrative Authentication
docs.text:
To interact with the API, requests must be authenticated using a valid GX-API-KEY. The system supports two primary delivery methods:

  1. Request Header (Recommended):
    • Key: GX-API-KEY
    • Value: your_secret_api_key_here
  2. Query String (Development only):
    • URL suffix: ?api-key=your_secret_api_key_here

docs.title: 🧭 Global Endpoint Structure
docs.text:
The GeniXCMS API follows standard RESTful naming conventions.

  • Base URL: https://yourdomain.com/api/v1/
  • Pattern: /{resource}/{identifier}

docs.title: 🏹 Standard Content Resources (Posts)
docs.text:
The posts resource is the primary interface for managing editorial content via the API.

Capability HTTP Method Endpoint Path List Records GET /api/v1/posts/ Fetch Single GET /api/v1/posts/{id} Create New POST /api/v1/posts/ Update Existing PUT /api/v1/posts/{id} Permanent Delete DELETE /api/v1/posts/{id}

docs.title: JSON Payload Example (POST/PUT)
docs.text:
{
    "title": "Post from Mobile App",
    "content": "<h1>Hello API</h1><p>Content body goes here...</p>",
    "status": "1",
    "type": "post",
    "author": "clark_kent"
}

docs.title: 📦 Consistent Response Architecture
docs.text: The API engine returns predictable JSON structures for both success and error states.

docs.title: Success Envelope (200 OK)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "message": "Resource retrieved successfully",
    "records": 42,
    "data": [ { ... }, { ... } ]
}

docs.title: Error Envelope (4xx / 5xx)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 404,
    "message": "The requested resource 'member/12' does not exist."
}

docs.title: 🚦 Rate Limiting & Quotas
docs.text: To ensure platform stability and protect against brute-force attacks, the GeniXCMS API implements a Sliding Window Rate Limiter.

docs.title: Default Quotas
docs.text:
By default, the system tracks requests per IP address over a 1-hour window.

  • Global Limit: Configurable via api_rate_limit (Default: 100 requests/hour).
  • Exceeded State: If a client exceeds the quota, the API returns a 429 Too Many Requests status code.

docs.title: HTTP Response Headers
docs.text:
Clients should monitor these headers to stay within their allocated budget:

Header Description X-RateLimit-Limit The maximum number of requests allowed in the current window. X-RateLimit-Remaining The number of requests remaining for the current IP. X-RateLimit-Reset The UTC timestamp (Unix) when the current window resets.

docs.title: 🛠️ Extensibility: Creating Custom Endpoints
docs.text:
Developers can easily register new API resources by creating a specialized controller class.

  • Storage Location: inc/lib/Control/Api/
  • Naming Convention: [Resource]Api.class.php
  • Routing: The Api Class automatically detects and routes the {resource} segment of the URL to your custom class.

[!TIP] API Key Management: You can generate and manage your secure keys in the Admin Dashboard > Settings > Security. Always keep your API keys confidential to prevent unauthorized content modifications.

[!IMPORTANT] Response Headers: The API engine automatically sets the Content-Type: application/json header and handles CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) pre-flight requests if configured in your security settings.

docs.title: ⚙️ Configuring the Rate Limiter
docs.text:
Rate limiting is fully configurable from the Admin Dashboard — no code changes required.

Location: Admin > Settings > Security > RESTful API KEY

Field Description Rate Limit (Req/Hour) Maximum number of requests allowed per IP per hour. Value: 0 Disables rate limiting entirely (open access). Value: 100 Default — allows 100 requests per IP per hour.

docs.title: 429 Error Response
docs.text:
When a client exceeds their quota, the API returns:

{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 429,
    "message": "Too many requests [Rate Limit Exceeded]"
}

[!CAUTION] Disable Only for Trusted Networks: Setting the rate limit to 0 effectively removes all request throttling. Only do this for private, internal APIs. Public-facing endpoints should always have a limit configured.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Security Settings — Configuring API keys and CORS policies.
  • Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the Posts API.

docs.title: Router (Smart URL Engine)
docs.text: The Router class is the core component responsible for parsing "Smart URLs" (Permalinks) and mapping them to specific system controllers and parameters. It supports robust, regex-based routing and allows for granular extension by third-party modules.

docs.title: 🗺️ Routing Architecture
docs.text: The router uses a predefined map of URI patterns that are matched against the incoming request. This system supports language prefixes, category structures, and custom module routes.

docs.title: 📜 Common Pattern Examples
docs.text: URI Pattern Parameter Result Description category/([0-9]+)/(.*)/ cat=ID Standard category archive archives. tag/(.*)/ tag=SLUG Tag-based content filtering. ([a-z]{2})/mod/(.*).html mod=NAME, lang=XX Module routes with language prefixes. rss/ rss Native RSS feed generation.

docs.title: Router::run()
docs.text:
The primary execution point of the routing engine. This method performs several critical tasks:

  1. URI Detection: Identifies the requested path, automatically handling installations in subdirectories.
  2. Pattern Matching: Compares the URI against the internal routing map.
  3. Parameter Extraction: Returns a structured array of matched parameters upon success.

docs.title: Router::add(array $routes)
docs.text:
Enables modules to register custom routing rules dynamically. New routes are automatically sorted to ensure that the most specific patterns are matched first.

// Registering a new custom route for a module
$my_route = [
    'product/([0-9]+)/' => [
        'product' => 1
    ]
];
Router::add($my_route);

docs.title: 🛠️ Specialized URL Handling
docs.text:
GeniXCMS's routing engine is designed with high flexibility to support various server environments and SEO requirements.

  • 🌍 Locale Support: The router automatically detects 2-letter ISO language codes (e.g., /en/, /id/) at the beginning of the URL and passes them as a lang parameter to the controller.
  • 📁 Subdirectory Intelligence: If GeniXCMS is installed in a folder (e.g., yoursite.com/cms/), the router strips the /cms/ prefix automatically before processing.
  • 🔗 Permalink Flexibility: Seamlessly falls back to index.php based routes if Apache's mod_rewrite (or equivalent Nginx configuration) is unavailable.

docs.title: ⚙️ URL Metadata & Suffixes
docs.text:
The routing engine respects the GX_URL_PREFIX constant (default: .html). This ensures that generated links and internal routes maintain consistent aesthetics across the entire application.

[!TIP] Regex Power: When adding custom routes, you can use capture groups (e.g., (.*)) and map them to their corresponding numerical indexes in your parameter array. This allows for highly dynamic and clean URL structures.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Control Layer — How the parameters returned by the router are dispatched to controllers.
  • Smart URL Guide — Configuring SEO-friendly URLs in the admin panel.

docs.title: Content Syndication Class (RSS)
docs.text: The RSS class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS that automates the generation of XML-compliant RSS 2.0 feeds. This enables your site's content to be syndicated to external readers, news aggregators, and email notification services.

docs.title: Rss::create(int $limit = 20, ...)
docs.text:
Compiles and directly outputs a formatted XML feed to the browser.

Parameter Type Default Description $limit int 20 Maximum number of items in the XML feed. $url_method string 'post' The Url Class method used for link generation. $type string 'post' The specific content type to include (e.g., post, page, catalog).

Returns: Directly streams XML content with the Content-Type: text/xml header.

// Typically executed within an RSS controller or dedicated feed route
Rss::create(50, 'post', 'post');

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The RSS class performs several automated data transformations to ensure XML validity and SEO performance.

  1. Metadata Discovery: Retrieves core site identity parameters (Title, URL, Description) from the Site and Options classes to populate the RSS Channel headers.
  2. Dataset Acquisition: Fetches recent content using the Posts Class's recent() method.
  3. Content Sanitization:
    • Tag Extraction: Strips internal GeniXCMS shortcodes like [[--readmore--]].
    • XML Escaping: Cleanses the content body of special characters that could break the XML parser.
    • Link Resolution: Uses the Url Class to ensure all links are absolute permalinks.

docs.title: 🏹 Automated Syndication Logic
docs.text:
When a request is made to your site's RSS endpoint (usually /rss.php or /feed/), the system: - Sets the appropriate XML Headers. - Sets the Language attribute based on your global multi-language settings. - Generates a Channel containing the most recent editorial content.

[!IMPORTANT] SEO Deployment: Always include the RSS feed link in your theme's <head> section to allow browsers and perimeters to auto-discover your syndicate: <link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" title="RSS Feed" href="{Site::$url}feed/">.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: RSS feeds are excellent candidates for Caching. Combined with the GeniXCMS cache engine, your feed can be delivered as a static XML file, significantly reducing server load during aggregator crawls.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the RSS engine.
  • Url Class — How the system generates absolute permalinks for feed items.
  • Cache Settings — Optimizing feed delivery performance.

docs.title: Security Infrastructure
docs.text: GeniXCMS implements a multi-layered security architecture designed to protect both the administration dashboard and custom themes from a wide range of web vulnerabilities. By enforcing modern standards like Content Security Policy (CSP), CSRF protection, and HTTP Header Hardening, the framework provides a secure-by-default environment for developers.

docs.title: 🛡️ Content Security Policy (CSP)
docs.text: CSP is the primary defense against Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. GeniXCMS dispatches a robust CSP header with every response.

docs.title: 📜 Default Policy Directives
docs.text: Directive Policy Description default-src 'self' Disallows all external resources by default. script-src 'self', 'unsafe-inline', trusted CDNs Allows internal scripts and whitelisted providers (JSDelivr, JQuery CDN, Cloudflare). style-src 'self', inline styles, trusted CDNs Allows internal CSS and whitelisted style providers. img-src 'self', data:, * (HTTPS) Allows internal images, base64 data URIs, and any secure external image. font-src 'self', data:, Google Fonts Allows internal fonts and trusted font providers (gstatic.com). object-src 'none' Completely disables plugins like Flash or Silverlight.

docs.title: 🔌 Extending the Whitelist
docs.text: If your custom theme or module requires resources from a new third-party CDN (e.g., Swiper, Chart.js, or Google Maps), you must register the domain using the system_security_headers_args filter hook.

docs.title: Example: Whitelisting a New API & CDN
docs.text:
Hooks::attach('system_security_headers_args', function($rules) {
    // Allow scripts from a new CDN
    $rules['script-src'][] = "https://cdn.my-library.com";

    // Allow AJAX/Fetch requests to an external API
    $rules['connect-src'][] = "https://api.my-data-source.com";

    return $rules;
});

docs.title: 🔒 HTTP Header Hardening
docs.text:
In addition to CSP, GeniXCMS automatically injects the following security-focused headers into every response:

  • X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff: Prevents browser-side MIME sniffing, forcing strict adherence to the declared Content-Type.
  • X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN: Protects against Clickjacking by preventing the site from being embedded in an <iframe> on external domains.
  • X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block: Enforces the built-in XSS filter in modern browsers.
  • Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin: Enhances user privacy by limiting referrer data sent to third-party sites.

docs.title: 🔑 CSRF Protection
docs.text:
Cross-Site Request Forgery is mitigated using a robust, token-based validation system.

  • Automated Generation: Managed by the Token class.
  • Controller Validation: Integrated into all core controllers.
  • Dashboard Constant: The TOKEN constant is globally available in the admin panel for easy form integration.

docs.title: Implementation Example:
docs.text:
<form action="/submit" method="POST">
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="{$token}">
    <!-- Form fields... -->
    <button type="submit">Complete Operation</button>
</form>

docs.title: 🛠️ Troubleshooting Violations
docs.text:
If you see "Refused to load..." or "Content Security Policy..." errors in your browser's developer console:

  1. Check Protocol: Ensure you are using https://. GeniXCMS blocks insecure http:// external assets by default.
  2. Verify Domain: Is the domain in our Default Whitelist? If not, use the system_security_headers_args hook.
  3. AJAX Connectivity: If your JavaScript fetch() or XMLHttpRequest is failing, ensure the API domain is added to the connect-src directive.

[!WARNING] While unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval are currently permitted for compatibility with legacy components (like elFinder), developers are encouraged to move towards nonce-based or hash-based script execution for custom developments.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Reference — For a deep dive into using policy filtering hooks.
  • Vendor Management — How integrated libraries are secured.

docs.title: Session Management & Storage Class
docs.text: The Session class is the primary state management engine for GeniXCMS. It provides a secure, abstraction layer for handling user sessions, offering support for both standard filesystem storage and persistent, high-performance database-backed sessions via the SessionHandlerInterface.

docs.title: Session::start(int $lifetime = 1)
docs.text:
Initializes the session engine with custom security parameters.

  • Security: Sets the httponly and samesite (Lax) cookie attributes to prevent Cross-Site Scripting (XSS).
  • Automation: Automatically groups all GeniXCMS session data under the $_SESSION['gx_sess'] namespace to avoid collisions with external modules.

docs.title: Session::set($key, $value = '')
docs.text:
Commits data to the active session.

Input Type Usage Description string Session::set('role', 'admin'); Sets a single key-value pair. array Session::set(['A' => '1', 'B' => '2']); Batch updates multiple session keys.

docs.title: Session::val(string $key)
docs.text:
Retrieves a specific value from the session namespace.

$is_authenticated = Session::val('is_logged_in');

docs.title: ⚙️ Persistent Database Sessions
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0-alpha supports offloading session storage to the database for improved reliability in load-balanced environments.

docs.title: Configuration (inc/config/config.php)
docs.text: Constant Default Description SESSION_DB false Set to true to store sessions in the sessions table. SESSION_EXPIRES 1 The session lifetime in hours.

docs.title: 🏗️ Lifecycle & Sanitation
docs.text:
The class implements the full SessionHandlerInterface for database interaction:

  • read() / write(): Decodes and encodes serialized session data into the sessions table.
  • gc(int $max_lifetime): Automated Garbage Collection that purges expired sessions to maintain database performance.
  • logout(): Destroys the entire session registry, clears local variables, and invalidates the session cookie.

docs.title: 🛠️ Security Best Practices
docs.text:
• Namespace Isolation: GeniXCMS uses the gx_sess prefix to ensure your site's authentication remains isolated from other applications on the same domain.
  • Encryption: When SESSION_DB is enabled, sensitive session data is stored in the database, reducing the risk of local session hijacking in shared hosting environments.

[!CAUTION] Session Destruction: Calling Session::logout() is irreversible for the current request. Ensure all required data has been persisted to the database before executing a logout command.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• User Class — How the authentication system interacts with sessions.
  • Db Class — The underlying database provider for persistent sessions.

docs.title: Global Website & SEO Class
docs.text: The Site class is the primary data container and orchestration engine for your website's global identity. It manages core site properties, automated SEO metadata generation, structured data (JSON-LD), and on-the-fly asset optimization, ensuring your frontend remains search-engine optimized and high-performing.

docs.title: ⚡ Core Identity Properties
docs.text:
The class provides static, global access to your site's foundational data.

Property Access Description Site::$name static The primary Website Name. Site::$url static The absolute Base URL (e.g., https://site.com/). Site::$desc static Global Meta Description for the homepage. Site::$slogan static Catchy marketing slogan or tagline. Site::$email static Administrative contact email address.

docs.title: 🌎 Automated SEO Engine
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0-alpha features an intelligent metadata aggregator that handles complex head-section logic.

docs.title: Site::meta(array $data)
docs.text:
Generates a comprehensive suite of SEO and social metadata tags.

  • Standard Tags: Charset, Generator, and Viewport.
  • SEO Optimization: Automated Page Titles, Meta Keywords, and Canonical URLs.
  • Social Graph: Generates Open Graph (Facebook) and Twitter Cards metadata based on post featured images and excerpts.
{* In your theme's header.php *}
{Site::meta($data)|noescape}

docs.title: Site::jsonLD(array $data)
docs.text:
Automatically generates Schema.org compliant JSON-LD scripts for search engine rich results.

  • BlogPosting: Injected on single post pages for improved article discovery.
  • WebPage: Injected on static pages for structural clarity.
  • Organization: Global schema for corporate/site identity.

docs.title: Site::logo(int $w, int $h, string $class)
docs.text:
Retrieves the site's primary logo with automated sizing and responsive class support.

// Render a 150px wide logo with a custom class
echo Site::logo(150, 0, 'navbar-brand-img');

docs.title: 🚀 Performance & Optimization
docs.text:
The Site class includes a high-performance Minification Engine for on-the-fly resource compression.

Method Role Logic minifyHTML($html) Compression Strips redundant whitespace and comments from the final rendered page. minifyCSS($css) Optimization Compresses raw CSS strings for faster transmission. minifyJS($js) Optimization Compresses JavaScript code for improved load times.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: The Site::title() helper automatically appends your site name to the page title (e.g., Blog Post | My Site) to ensure consistent branding across all search engine results.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Options Class — How core site properties are persistently stored.
  • Router Class — How the system resolves absolute URLs.
  • General Settings — Configuring site identity in the dashboard.

docs.title: Search Engine Sitemap Class
docs.text: The Sitemap class is a specialized utility in GeniXCMS designed to automate the generation of XML-compliant Sitemaps. This functionality is critical for modern SEO, as it provides search engine crawlers with a structured map of your site's content, ensuring all posts, pages, and module-specific resources are indexed accurately.

docs.title: ⚡ Index & Map Generation
docs.text: GeniXCMS supports both individual content maps and master sitemap indexes for larger installations.

docs.title: Sitemap::createIndex()
docs.text:
Generates a master Sitemap Index file. This is the primary entry point for search engine crawlers (e.g., sitemap.xml).

  • Logic: It dynamically lists available content-type sitemaps (Posts, Pages, Categories).
  • Format: Directly streams XML with the Content-Type: text/xml header.

docs.title: Sitemap::create(string $type, ...)
docs.text:
Generates an individual content-specific map list.

Parameter Type Default Description $type string 'post' The specific content type to include (e.g., post, page). $limit int 20 Maximum number of items in the XML feed. $url_method string 'post' The Url Class method used for link generation. 
// Typically executed within a sitemap controller or dedicated route
Sitemap::create('post', 100, 'post');

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture
docs.text:
The Sitemap class performs automated data transformations to ensure XML compliance and SEO efficacy.

  1. Metric Acquisition: Fetches recent content updates using the Posts Class.
  2. Metadata Injection:
    • lastmod: Automatically calculates the last modification date based on the post timestamp.
    • changefreq: Defaults to daily to encourage frequent re-crawling.
    • priority: Assigns relative priority to different content types (Home > Post > Category).
  3. Link Resolution: Uses the Url Class to ensure all links are absolute permalinks, including Smart URL support.

docs.title: 🔌 Extensibility: Registering Custom Maps
docs.text:
Modules can register their own custom content types into the sitemap engine using the Sitemap::addMap() method.

// Registering a Custom Product Module Sitemap
$new_map = [
    'product' => [
        'class' => 'ProductUrl', // The class handling the URL logic
        'url'   => 'detail'      // The specific method to call
    ]
];
Sitemap::addMap($new_map);

[!IMPORTANT] Crawler Submission: Once your sitemap is live (usually at /sitemap.xml or /sitemap/), ensure you submit the URL to Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools to jumpstart your site's index discovery.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: Sitemaps are excellent candidates for Caching. Large sitemaps with thousands of entries should be cached as static XML files to prevent unnecessary database load during crawler visits.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Url Class — How the system generates absolute permalinks for the sitemap.
  • Posts Class — The underlying model consumed by the map engine.
  • Cache Settings — Optimizing XML delivery performance.

docs.title: System Analytics & Statistics Class
docs.text: The Stats class is the primary data-aggregation engine for GeniXCMS. It provides a comprehensive set of methods for gathering system-wide intelligence, including content volume, user growth metrics, and advanced post-view tracking with built-in heuristic bot detection.

docs.title: ⚡ Content Metrics
docs.text:
The following methods are critical for populating administration dashboards and site-wide report summaries.

Method Role Logic totalPost($type) Volume Total record count for the specified type (e.g., post, page). activePost($type) Production Counts only published and public records (Status 1). inactivePost($type) Moderation Counts drafts, hidden, or scheduled records (Status 0). totalCat($type) Taxonomy Counts all active categories for the specified context.

docs.title: 👥 User Engagement Analytics
docs.text:
Monitor your community's growth and administrative overhead.

  • Stats::totalUser(): Returns the total number of registered general members.
  • Stats::totalAdmin(): Returns the count of accounts with Level 0 (Administrator) access.
  • Stats::activeUser(): Counts verified and active user profiles.
  • Stats::pendingUser(): Counts users currently awaiting email verification steps.

docs.title: 📈 Content Popularity Tracking
docs.text: GeniXCMS features a robust, human-focused view tracking system.

docs.title: Stats::addViews(int $post_id)
docs.text:
Increments the views counter for a specific content record.

  • Bot Protection: The method automatically filters and discards requests from over 50 common search engine crawlers (Googlebot, Bingbot, etc.) to ensure your analytics represent real human engagement.
  • Implementation: Typically called at the beginning of your theme's single.php.

docs.title: Stats::mostViewed(int $limit, ...)
docs.text:
Retrieves a collection of post objects ranked by their accumulated view counts.

// Fetch the top 5 most popular blog posts
$trending = Stats::mostViewed(5, 'post');

docs.title: 💬 Comment & Interaction Metrics
docs.text:
Method Description totalComments() Cumulative count of all reader submissions. activeComments() Counts approved and live comments. pendingComments() Counts submissions currently held in the moderation queue.

[!IMPORTANT] Performance Architecture: The Stats class methods are optimized to perform single-metric SQL queries. When building heavy report dashboards, consider Caching the returned values if your database volume is extremely high.

[!TIP] Dashboard Utility: Use these methods in your custom Programmable Dashboard widgets to provide your clients with real-time site updates.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Posts Class — The model where content metrics are derived.
  • Dashboard Guide — How these metrics appear in the UI.
  • Programmable Dashboard — Building custom reporting widgets.

docs.title: Bootstrap Core (System) Class
docs.text: The System class is the foundational bootstrap core of GeniXCMS. It is responsible for the entire orchestration of the application lifecycle — from low-level environment checks and dependency loading to module registration, session initialization, and final output buffering.

docs.title: 🏗️ The Bootstrap Sequence
docs.text:
The System constructor initializes the framework in a strict, sequential order to ensure all dependencies are available when needed.

  1. Environment Setup: Defines core constants and verifies mandatory PHP extensions.
  2. Configuration Load: Retrieves and validates the config.php credentials.
  3. Core Initialization: Directs the startup of Database, Hooks, Options, and Language engines.
  4. Extension Discovery: Scans and registers all active Modules and the current Theme.
  5. Session Logic: Triggers Session::start() to restore user state.
  6. Initialization Hook: Executes the init action, allowing modules to latch onto the startup process.

docs.title: System::lib(string $class)
docs.text:
A secure library loader for manual dependency injection.

  • Storage: Automatically looks for .class.php files within the inc/lib/ directory.
  • Usage: System::lib('MyCustomLib');

docs.title: System::alert(array $data)
docs.text:
Dispatches elegant notification toasts to the administrative dashboard.

Key Visual Style Best Use Case alertSuccess Green (Success) Completed transactions and settings saves. alertDanger Red (Error) Validations failures and database errors. alertWarning Yellow (Warning) Security notices and configuration tips. 
$notification = [
    'alertSuccess' => ['User profile updated successfully.'],
    'alertWarning' => ['Please check your SMTP settings.']
];
System::alert($notification);

docs.title: System::v()
docs.text: Returns the semantic version string of the current installation (e.g., 2.0.0-alpha).

docs.title: System::versionCheck()
docs.text:
Orchestrates an asynchronous integrity check against the official GeniXCMS repository.

  • Alerting: If a critical update is detected, an alert is automatically pinned to the administrative header.
  • Frequency: These checks are performed periodically to ensure minimal infrastructure overhead.

docs.title: 🚀 Performance & UI Tools
docs.text:
• GZip Acceleration: The System class handles mid-tier compression to reduce page transfer times.
  • System::toolbar(string $mode): Provides pre-configured JSON setups for the integrated Summernote editor (mini, light, or full).

[!IMPORTANT] Kernel Integrity: The System class should never be manually modified by theme or module developers. All custom startup logic should be implemented via the init hook: Hooks::add_action('init', 'my_custom_init_function');.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Hooks Class — How the bootstrap process exposes hooks to extensions.
  • Options Class — How the system loads its boot configuration.
  • Admin Dashboard — Where system alerts and version notices appear.

docs.title: Post Tagging & Taxonomy Class
docs.text: The Tags class is a specialized extension of the GeniXCMS taxonomy system. While it leverages the underlying table structure of the Categories class, it provides specific logic for managing flat descriptors (Tags), batch parsing, and the dynamic generation of "Tag Cloud" UI components for your themes.

docs.title: Tags::add(string $tags_list)
docs.text:
Parses and commits a collection of tag entities to the database.

  • Automation: The method automatically handles comma-separated strings, de-duplicates entries, and checks for existence before performing an INSERT.
  • Logic: It ensures all entries are assigned the tag type in the database.
// Batch insert newly discovered tags
Tags::add("industrial, premium, latte, vite");

docs.title: Tags::delete(int $tag_id)
docs.text: Permanently removes a tag and its associated metadata from the system.

docs.title: Tags::cloud()
docs.text:
Generates a semantic HTML Tag Cloud suitable for theme sidebars or footer widgets.

  • Process: Fetches all entities of type tag, calculates the frequency of each using Tags::count(), and returns a sorted list of linked items.
{* In your theme's sidebar.php *}
<div class="widget tag-cloud">
    {Tags::cloud()|noescape}
</div>

docs.title: 🏗️ Technical Architecture & Wrapper Methods
docs.text:
The Tags class acts as a high-level wrapper for several Categories Class utilities, pre-configured for the tag context.

Method Role Logic dropdown() Selection UI Generates a <select> list of all active tags. name($id) Identity Resolves the plain-text name for a specific tag ID. slug($id) Routing Returns the SEO-friendly URL slug for the tag. lists() Collection Returns an raw array of all tag objects.

docs.title: 📂 Database Schema
docs.text:
Tags are stored in the core cat table, distinguished by the type field value set to 'tag'. This unified architecture allows tags to benefit from the same robust slug-generation and multi-language support as standard categories.

[!TIP] SEO Performance: Use Categories for broad, hierarchical groupings (e.g., News, Products) and use Tags for specific attributes (e.g., PHP, Vite, White). This creates a logical, data-rich map for search engine spiders.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Categories Class — The underlying taxonomy engine.
  • Posts Class — How tags are associated with content records.
  • User Guide: Categories — Managing your site's taxonomy in the dashboard.

docs.title: Presentation & Theme Architecture Class
docs.text: The Theme class is the primary orchestrator for the visual presentation layer of GeniXCMS. It manages the discovery, activation, and rendering of themes, providing standardized methods for including structural components (Headers, Footers, Sidebars) and handling theme-specific logic through function.php and options.php integrations.

docs.title: ⚡ Structural Rendering Methods
docs.text: GeniXCMS themes follow a modular architecture. These methods allow you to bridge your PHP logic with your presentation templates.

docs.title: Theme::header($data) / Theme::footer($data)
docs.text:
These are the foundational entry/exit points for every theme file.

  • header(): Sends SEO meta-tags and cache headers, then includes the theme's header.php.
  • footer(): Includes footer.php and executes the footer_load_lib hook for library injection.
  • Data Flow: The $data array passed to these methods is globally accessible within the theme files.

docs.title: Theme::theme(string $file, ...)
docs.text:
A secure loader for including sub-templates from the active theme directory.

// Includes: inc/themes/{active_theme}/sidebars/main-blog.php
Theme::theme('sidebars/main-blog');

docs.title: 🛠️ Theme Infrastructure & Logic
docs.text:
The Theme class facilitates the separation of design and business logic through specialized entry points.

File Role Automated Loading function.php Custom helper functions and hooks. Loaded automatically during System boot. options.php The theme's administration settings panel. Included via the Admin > Appearance menu. themeinfo.php Metadata (Name, Version, Author). Parsed by Theme::data() for the dashboard.

docs.title: 🏗️ Administrative Management
docs.text:
These methods are primarily used by the core dashboard to manage the theme lifecycle.

  • Theme::activate(string $name): Sets the specified folder as the system's active theme.
  • Theme::thmList(): Scans the inc/themes/ directory for valid theme packages.
  • Theme::data(string $name): Extracts versioning and credit information from a theme's metadata file.

docs.title: Theme::editor(string $mode, ...)
docs.text:
Configures and injects the Summernote WYSIWYG editor for use within theme-driven forms or module interfaces.

  • Modes: mini, light, or full toolsets.
// Render a compact editor in your theme's frontend form
Theme::editor('mini', 200);

docs.title: 📂 Internal Constants
docs.text:
Upon initialization, the class defines the THEME constant, which contains the folder name of the currently active theme. This is used system-wide for absolute path resolution.

[!TIP] Performance Hint: To ensure your theme remains high-performing, always use Theme::theme() instead of standard PHP include. This method uses an internal registry to prevent redundant disk I/O when including the same component multiple times.

[!IMPORTANT] Theme Security: Never hardcode your theme's path. Always use the Url Class: Url::theme() to retrieve the public-facing URL of your theme's assets (CSS, JS, Images).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Url Class — Retrieving absolute paths for theme assets.
  • OptionsBuilder Class — Building professional settings panels within options.php.
  • Theme User Guide — Managing appearance from the dashboard.

docs.title: Security Token (CSRF Protection) Class
docs.text: The Token class is a critical security layer in GeniXCMS, specifically designed to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) attacks. It manages the lifecycle of unique, non-guessable security tokens that must be included in every state-changing request (POST, PUT, DELETE) throughout the administrative and frontend areas.

docs.title: 🛡️ Core Protection Architecture
docs.text:
GeniXCMS implements a "Locked URL" token strategy.

  1. Global Constant: Upon system initialization, the engine generates a new token and assigns it to the TOKEN global constant.
  2. State Pairing: Each token is cryptographically paired with the specific URL that generated it. This prevents a token from one administrative page from being used on another unauthorized endpoint.

docs.title: Token::create()
docs.text:
Generates and commits a new security handshake to the internal registry.

  • Process: Captures a 10-character random string, the current request's URL, the visitor's IP address, and a high-precision timestamp.
  • Storage: Tokens are JSON-serialized and persistently stored in the options table under the tokens key for cross-request availability.

docs.title: Token::isValid(string $token)
docs.text:
The primary validation method for incoming state changes.

  • Verification: It checks for the token's presence in the registry AND ensures that the current request URL matches the original "pairing" hash generated during creation.
  • Returns: bool (True if the handshake is valid and matching).

docs.title: 🎨 Form Implementation Pattern
docs.text: To secure any HTML form, include the token as a hidden input field.

docs.title: Frontend (HTML)
docs.text:
<form action="save_profile.php" method="POST">
    <!-- CSRF Protection Field -->
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="<?= TOKEN; ?>">

    <label>Username</label>
    <input type="text" name="username">
    <button type="submit">Save Changes</button>
</form>

docs.title: Backend (PHP Controller)
docs.text:
if (Http::isPost()) {
    if (!Token::isValid($_POST['token'])) {
        // Log potential attack and terminate
        die("Security Breach Detected: Invalid or Expired Token.");
    }
    // Proceed with safe processing...
}

docs.title: 🏗️ Lifecycle & Garbage Collection
docs.text:
• Temporal Expiration: To maintain a lean database and ensure high security, the ridOld() method automatically purges tokens older than 24 hours.
  • User Isolation: Tokens are associated with specific user sessions and IPs, preventing hijacked tokens from being effective on external networks.

[!CAUTION] Ajax Security: When building custom module interfaces using Ajax, you must include the token parameter in your data payload or as a header. Failure to do so will result in a 403 Security Error if your backend validates the request.

[!IMPORTANT] Smart URL Compatibility: The token pairing logic is aware of Smart URL rewrites. It uses the canonicalized URL provided by the Router Class to ensure consistent validation across different URL formats.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Http Class — Handling request methods (POST/GET).
  • Router Class — How URLs are resolved for token pairing.
  • Security Settings — Managing global security policies.

docs.title: Typography & Security Sanitization Class
docs.text: The Typo class is the primary security filter and data manipulation engine for GeniXCMS. It provides comprehensive utilities for input sanitization (XSS protection), cryptographic token generation, URI slugification, and automated content formatting.

docs.title: 🛡️ Security & Sanitization
docs.text: GeniXCMS implements a "Sanitize on Input, Escape on Output" philosophy using the following methods:

docs.title: Typo::cleanX(string $input)
docs.text:
The primary, high-level sanitizer used for every form submission in the dashboard.

  • Process: Employs HTMLPurifier to recursively strip malicious JavaScript, handles deep HTML encoding, and executes secondary XSS attribute filtering.
  • Best Use Case: Processing post content, comments, and site settings.

docs.title: Typo::filterXss(string $raw_html)
docs.text: A lightweight, fast filter targeted specifically at removing dangerous event listeners (e.g., onclick, alert) and URI schemes (e.g., javascript:, data:) while preserving safe HTML structure.

docs.title: ⚡ Data Manipulation & Slugs
docs.text:
Convert raw text into SEO-friendly identifiers or legible document fragments.

Method Role Logic slugify($text) SEO Utility Transforms "Hello World!" into "hello-world" with ASCII normalization. url2link($text) Auto-Linker Scans content for URIs and automatically wraps them in <a> tags. nl2p($text) Formatting Converts double-newlines into semantic <p> paragraphs. translate($str) Localization Wrapper for the internal _() Gettext localization engine.

docs.title: 🔐 Cryptography & Handshakes
docs.text:
The Typo class provides several layers of randomness for security tokens and password resets.

  • Typo::getToken(int $len): Generates a standard, high-entropy alphanumeric token.
  • Typo::crypto_rand_secure($min, $max): Returns a cryptographically secure random integer suitable for sensitive operations.
  • Typo::createToken(...): An advanced generator with granular control over uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols.

docs.title: ✅ Validation Utilities
docs.text:
• Typo::validateEmail(string $email): Returns a bool based on strict RFC format verification.
  • Typo::int($var): Force casts and cleanses a variable into a valid integer.
  • Typo::float($var): Formats a numerical value as a float with 2 decimal precision.

[!CAUTION] Data Integrity: When using Typo::Xclean() to decode content for editing, remember that the data is no longer "safe" for direct browser rendering. Always re-apply Typo::cleanX() before committing the edited content back to the database.

[!IMPORTANT] Localisation Integrity: The Typo::translate() method relies on the theme's .mo or .po files. If a translation is missing, it will gracefully fall back to the original English string.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Token Class — How typo tokens are used for CSRF protection.
  • Database Class — How sanitized data is persistently stored.
  • Language Class — Managing multi-lingual site content.

docs.title: Administration UI Builder Class
docs.text: The UiBuilder class is a sophisticated, high-level layout engine introduced in GeniXCMS v2.0.0-alpha. It is designed to construct professional, consistent, and responsive administrative interfaces — including dashboards, complex forms, data tables, and interactive stat cards — using a declarative array-based Schema approach.

docs.title: ✨ Modern ERP Interface Features
docs.text:
• 📂 Sticky Header System: Automated, top-pinned navigation fragments with breadcrumbs, state-aware icons, and primary action buttons.
  • 🏗️ Declarative Layouts: Define rows, columns, and nested cards using pure PHP arrays, eliminating the need for manual HTML/Bootstrap classes.
  • 🎨 Component Library: Native support for Stat Cards (with trend indicators), Switches (iOS-style toggles), and Contextual Alerts.
  • 📱 Responsive by Default: Automatically calculates Bootstrap 5 grid widths to ensure a seamless experience on tablets and desktops.

docs.title: 🏗️ Building an Interface
docs.text:
To implement the builder in your module's backend view, define a nested schema and trigger the rendering engine.

// 1. Define the UI Schema
$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title'    => _('Inventory Manager'),
        'subtitle' => _('Track and manage physical assets.'),
        'icon'     => 'bi bi-box-seam-fill',
        'button'   => [ // Global action button
            'type'  => 'button', 
            'name'  => 'add_item', 
            'label' => _('New Entry'),
            'class' => 'btn btn-primary rounded-pill'
        ]
    ],
    'content' => [
        ['type' => 'row', 'items' => [
            ['width' => 8, 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'card', 'title' => 'General Info', 'body_elements' => [
                    ['type' => 'input', 'name' => 'sku', 'label' => 'Product SKU'],
                    ['type' => 'switch', 'name' => 'is_active', 'label' => 'Publish Status']
                ]]
            ]],
            ['width' => 4, 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'stats_card', 'label' => 'Total Stock', 'value' => '1,240', 'icon' => 'bi bi-archive']
            ]]
        ]]
    ]
];

// 2. Instantiate and Render
$ui = new UiBuilder($schema);
$ui->render();

docs.title: 📝 Available UI Elements
docs.text:
The UiBuilder abstracts standard HTML into high-level components:

Element Visual Role Key Parameters card Content Container title, icon, body_elements (array). stats_card Analytics Metric label, value, icon, trend (up/down). switch Boolean Toggle name, label, checked (bool). input Data Entry name, label, type (text/email/etc). alert Inline Notice type (success/danger/info), message. row Grid Wrapper items (holds nested columns).

docs.title: ⚡ Specialized Rendering Methods
docs.text:
The builder allows for granular control over the output process:

  • renderHeader(): Compiles and outputs only the top-level sticky header.
  • renderTabs(): Dynamically builds sub-navigation tabs if defined in the schema.
  • render(): The primary entry point that assembles the entire interface.
